Military Mar 26, 2026

Why The World’s Most-Produced Fighter Jet May Finally Disappear In 2026

Why the last combat-worthy MiG-21s likely disappeared in 2025, with only on-paper rusting hulks in service in 2026.

The MiG-21's retirement timeline reveals a critical gap in modernization capabilities for developing nations that lack resources for fifth-generation fighter replacement.

See Simple Flying →

What goes on behind the scenes?

Understanding concealed aircraft systems like avionics bays and hydraulic compartments helps crews diagnose maintenance issues and ensures operational safety compliance.

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Organizations must document their rationale for accepting residual risks that remain after implementing feasible controls, creating accountability for safety trade-offs.

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA Ames Experts Available for Artemis II Flight Test Interviews

NASA’s Ames Research Center in Silicon Valley invites media to interview local subject matter experts on Friday, March 27 from 10 a.m. to 2 p.m. ahead of the agency sending astronauts around the Moon for the first time in more than 50 years with the Artemis II flight test. NASA teams are gearing up...

NASA Ames is positioning Silicon Valley expertise as a key communication resource for Artemis II, signaling the program's reliance on distributed technical knowledge beyond traditional Houston mission control.

See NASA →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

5 Airlines With The Largest Widebody Fleets In North America

All five are based in the United States.

Widebody fleet concentration among US carriers reflects their dominance in long-haul international routes and hub-and-spoke network strategies across North America.

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA Sets Coverage for First Artemis Crewed Mission Around Moon

A variety of prelaunch, launch, and mission events for NASA’s Artemis II mission around the Moon will stream online. The agency is targeting no earlier than Wednesday, April 1, for the test flight during a two-hour window that opens at 6:24 p.m. EDT, with additional launch opportunities through...

NASA's April 2025 Artemis II lunar mission represents the critical human spaceflight test preceding Moon landings, with public streaming access enabling unprecedented transparency for a flagship government space program.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

Space Force weighs launch alternatives as Vulcan faces potential months-long grounding

Officials explore shifting missions, extending satellites already in orbit

Space Force contingency planning for potential Vulcan delays reveals systemic dependence on single-source launch providers, exposing vulnerability in national security space operations.

Regulatory Mar 26, 2026

SMS Declaration of Compliance

What the SMS Declaration of Compliance means for operators and what the FAA expects.

Operators must formally document their Safety Management System compliance to the FAA, shifting from informal practices to verifiable, auditable safety governance structures.

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Delta Air Lines Continues Los Angeles Lead, Schedules More Flights To Florida This Winter

Delta is adding more Florida flying from LAX as its battle with United intensifies.

Delta's expansion of LAX-Florida service signals escalating capacity competition on high-yield leisure routes, potentially pressuring yields industry-wide.

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

JetBlue Considers Selling Itself To A Major US Carrier, Weighs How Washington Will React

JetBlue's exploration of a potential sale to rival airlines has sparked intense speculation and antitrust scrutiny.

JetBlue's sale consideration signals consolidation pressure in US aviation despite regulatory headwinds that could block deals competitors have faced.

Up To 22-Hour Flights: United Airlines' 10 New Ultra-Long Routes In 2026

Ultra-long routes demand fleet reconfiguration and crew scheduling innovations that could reshape operational economics for carriers pursuing point-to-point network strategies.

Airlines are increasingly differentiating premium economy as a distinct cabin class to capture price-sensitive business travelers unwilling to pay full business fares.

RNO Mustang 9 Departure

Pilots departing from runways 17 left and right at Reno-Tahoe International need to pay special attention to the Mustang 9 Departure. It is extremely important that the procedure on the Mustang 9...

The Mustang 9 departure from Reno-Tahoe's parallel runways requires heightened pilot attention due to specific procedural complexity that differs from standard departures.

NASA Sets Coverage for Artemis II Moon Mission

Editor’s note: NASA will continuously update this Artemis II briefings and mission events page throughout prelaunch, launch, and mission activities. NASA will provide live coverage of prelaunch, launch, and mission events for the agency’s upcoming Artemis II crewed test flight around the Moon....

NASA's continuous coverage strategy for Artemis II demonstrates the agency's commitment to public transparency during critical crewed lunar missions, enabling real-time stakeholder engagement throughout all mission phases.

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

Artemis Moon Tree Dedicated in Honor of Mary W. Jackson

On March 18, 2026, students, staff, and members of NASA’s Langley Research Center gathered at Mary W. Jackson Elementary School in Hampton to celebrate the dedication of a remarkable addition to the campus – an Artemis Moon Tree. Although formally dedicated on this day, the loblolly pine had...

NASA's Artemis Moon Tree initiative connects space exploration achievements to educational institutions, inspiring the next generation of aerospace professionals through symbolic, tangible links to lunar missions.

See NASA →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Why Emirates Doesn't Want The Boeing 737 MAX

The airline has little interest in this popular narrowbody.

Emirates' preference for widebody aircraft reflects a strategic focus on long-haul, high-yield routes where the 737 MAX's narrowbody economics don't align with their business model.

Southwest Airlines Passenger Blasts New Policy After Being Required To Buy An Extra Seat

One passenger's ordeal at the airport in March 2026 raises questions about Southwest's 'Customer of Size' policy and its impact on travelers.

Southwest's enforcement of seating policies on plus-size passengers risks regulatory scrutiny and potential discrimination claims as airlines balance revenue with accessibility standards.

Safety Mar 26, 2026

Flying in the Unforgiving Wire Environment (The Rotorcraft Collective)

Many pilots mistakenly believe they will have sufficient reaction time if they encounter power lines and other wires. Yet wire strikes have been among the top three causes of fatal helicopter...

Wire strikes remain a leading helicopter fatality cause despite pilots' overconfidence in reaction time, requiring enhanced detection systems and operational protocols.

US House passes bill to clear way for overland supersonic flights

The US House of Representatives passed legislation aimed at opening the skies to civil supersonic flight over land,…

FAA will gain authority to establish sound standards for overland supersonic routes, potentially unlocking a multi-billion dollar market currently restricted by 1970s noise regulations.

Eve flies eVTOL prototype for Brazilian president in high-profile test milestone

Eve Air Mobility flew its full-scale engineering prototype for Brazilian authorities, including President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva,…

Eve's presidential demonstration signals critical regulatory validation progress in Brazil, positioning the nation as a potential early-adopter market for eVTOL commercial operations.

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

How Many McDonnell Douglas MD-11 Aircraft Are In Service?

The jet has mostly disappeared from our skies.

The MD-11's decline reflects how technological obsolescence and fuel inefficiency can render even advanced aircraft uncompetitive within two decades of service.

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA Names Jennifer Lyons Acting Launch Services Program Manager

NASA has selected Jennifer Lyons as acting program manager for the agency’s Launch Services Program (LSP) based at Kennedy Space Center in Florida. In this role, Lyons will lead NASA’s acquisition and management of domestic commercial launch services for science and robotic exploration missions...

NASA's LSP leadership transition signals potential shifts in how the agency prioritizes and contracts with commercial launch providers for its science missions.

See NASA →

Industry Mar 26, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across all aviation layers—not just aircraft design—offers immediate efficiency gains to meet capacity demands while reducing environmental impact before new technologies mature.

See MDPI →

Drones Mar 25, 2026

BRINC Unveils Guardian, Launching the Next Era of Drone as First Responder

Guardian delivers 24/7 operations, unmatched imaging, Starlink connectivity, and more to help public safety agencies

First responder drones now offer continuous operations with satellite connectivity, fundamentally expanding emergency response capabilities beyond visual line-of-sight limitations.

See sUAS News →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

Office of Space Commerce releases mission authorization proposal

The Office of Space Commerce has rolled out its proposal for a “light touch” approach to mission authorization, the latest attempt in an effort to regulate new commercial space applications.

A streamlined authorization framework could accelerate commercial space venture launches by reducing regulatory friction while maintaining safety oversight mechanisms.

See SpaceNews →

Automating debrief analysis could accelerate pilot proficiency development while freeing instructors to focus on personalized coaching gaps.

Textron Aviation To Showcase Full Range Of Aircraft At Sun ’n Fun

Textron Aviation will display Cessna, Beechcraft, and Pipistrel aircraft together for the first time at Sun ’n Fun 2026.

Textron's unified display of three aircraft brands signals a strategic shift toward integrated product marketing across its general aviation portfolio.

Cirrus posts strong 2025 with 691 SR Series deliveries, 106 Vision Jets

Cirrus Aircraft reported it delivered 691 SR Series piston aircraft and a record 106 Vision Jets in 2025,…

Cirrus's Vision Jet record sales signal accelerating transition from piston to jet propulsion in general aviation's premium segment.

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

Webb Captures Saturn in Infrared

Saturn’s icy rings glow in this infrared view from NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope released on March 25, 2026. Combining this image with another that NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope captured – also released on the same day – provides scientists with a richer, more layered understanding of the gas...

Webb's infrared Saturn observations demonstrate how space telescope technology advances planetary science capabilities beyond visible-light analysis.

Study Unveils Redevelopment Concepts For Burke Lakefront Airport

A new economic study outlines two potential mixed-use redevelopment plans for Burke Lakefront Airport, part of Mayor Justin Bibb’s effort to close the city-operated airport.

Municipal airports facing closure can leverage economic studies to attract alternative development investment, potentially offsetting aviation operations revenue loss.

Terran Orbital Introduces New Star Tracker Product Line at SATSHOW 2026

Lockheed Martin's satellite attitude determination technology expansion signals intensifying competition in the growing commercial satellite constellation market.

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA Webb, Hubble Share Most Comprehensive View of Saturn to Date

NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope and Hubble Space Telescope have teamed up to capture new views of Saturn, revealing the planet in strikingly different ways. Observing in complementary wavelengths of light, the two space observatories provide scientists with a richer, more layered understanding of...

Multi-wavelength space telescope observations demonstrate how complementary sensor technologies enhance planetary science data fidelity for atmospheric research applications.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA-JAXA’s XRISM Telescope Clocks Hot Wind of Galaxy M82

For the first time, astronomers have directly measured the speed of superheated gas billowing from a cauldron of stellar activity at the heart of M82.

Advanced space telescope collaborations demonstrate measurement capabilities that could inform future aerospace thermal management and propulsion system design principles.
